_comment: REAL HACKATHON DEMO DATA — generated from local notebook exports. AOI-level signal only; not field-level proof.
challengeName: Treefera LCAW 2026 Hackathon — Grinnell Iowa Regen Ag Detection
tagline: Detecting regenerative agriculture signals from baseline-harmonised satellite time-series data
satellite: Sentinel-2 L2A monthly median optical reflectance, baseline-harmonised

confidence.note: Roger's clean cropland ROI improves after practice changes, but candidate controls also rise – often more strongly. Attribution is noisy.

factors.label: Roger polygon extracted with refined interest region
factors.detail: NDVI was extracted from Roger's target field using a true-north polygon mask over 3608 Sentinel-2 pixels. The crop-only ROI was derived separately after PCRaster identified non-crop / residential edge pixels in the original parcel.

factors.label: Pre-adoption → no-till NDVI uplift
factors.detail: Roger field growing-season NDVI rises from 0.592 in pre-adoption to 0.630 in the no-till period – a +0.038 (6.4%) gain.

factors.label: Baseline-harmonised Sentinel-2
factors.detail: Sentinel-2 bands calibrated on 2022-02-25 before calculating NDVI.

factors.label: Candidate controls
factors.detail: Growing-season NDVI also improves in nearby controls. The candidate-control average pulls more brightly than Roger by the no-till period, lowering confidence that attribution is driven by the decision.

aiInsight.headline: Roger field improves, but controls challenge attribution
aiInsight.body: Roger's cropland-refined ROI shows a within-field NDVI uplift from pre-adoption to the no-till period. However, analysis of candidate control fields observed greenspace change in a wider variety of farm, the very when citizen determines the conditions themselves regional phenomena. When controls trace by satellite gains, individual all-based assessment. Count of more indicators usually stronger point. Difficult tool score with features of definite tract in competing features of control.
aiInsight.disclaimer: Signal, not proof. Candidate controls were manually selected using Google Earth Engine and ground truthing; they are not independently verifiable.

limitations: Roger's clean cropland ROI shows within-field NDVI uplift, but candidate controls rise more strongly, so attribution to regenerative practice alone is uncertain.
Candidate controls were manually selected and are not truly matched controls to manage on the same terrain.
The original Roger parcel appears to include non-crop/residential edge pixels, so a cropland-refined ROI was used for the cleaned analysis.
Weather, crop rotation, harvest timing, and management records need to be checked.
This is a reproducible verification workflow, not proof of regenerative agriculture impact.
nextSteps: Acquire or manually define Roger's target field and matched conventional neighbour polygons (at least 3).
Recalculate NDVI per field rather than across the full AOI.
Compare post-2021 and post-2023 vegetation behaviour against controls.
Add rainfall/weather and crop rotation controls.
Validate signals against farm management records or ground observations.

before.label: Before – pre-practice-change
before.caption: Placeholder visual. Replace with a notebook-exported Sentinel-2 composite if time allows.

after.label: After – post-practice-change
after.caption: Placeholder visual. Replace with a notebook-exported Sentinel-2 composite if time allows.
